# Instructions for creating live-translation Electron Application

## Features
- Translates speech audio to text in a different language
  - User selects translation direction: either Japanese→English OR English→Japanese
  - Settings allow switching between the two directions
  - Only one translation direction active at a time
- Utilize Web Speech API (webkitSpeechRecognition) for speech recognition
  - **Input Speech Display and Verification**
    - Display recognized speech in real-time as user speaks
    - Show interim/partial results while listening (grayed out for distinction)
    - Display final recognized text once speech recognition completes
    - Input language badge/indicator showing which language is being recognized
    - Visual indicator distinguishing interim vs. final results
    - Show confidence score/accuracy percentage from speech recognition
    - Display microphone recording indicator/animation while listening
    - Show recognized text in a dedicated input panel above translation output
    - Highlight text being actively recognized with visual feedback
    - Copy button to copy recognized text to clipboard
- Utilize Google Translate API via Google Apps Script for translation
- Capture audio stream from multiple sources with user control
  - **Microphone Input**: Capture speech directly from system microphone
    - Support automatic gain control (AGC)
    - Noise suppression/echo cancellation
    - Adjustable microphone sensitivity levels
  - **System Audio**: Capture audio from system speakers/output device
    - Monitor system sounds for translation
    - Works across Windows, macOS, and Linux
    - Handle multiple audio devices
  - **Application Audio**: Capture audio from specific applications
    - Select target application for audio capture
    - Allow mixing microphone with application audio
    - Real-time audio level monitoring
  - **Audio Input Selection Panel**
    - Dropdown menu to switch between audio sources
    - Display active audio device/application
    - Show audio level meters in real-time
    - Allow user to test microphone input before recording
  - **Advanced Audio Options**
    - Volume/gain adjustment slider
    - Audio format selection (mono/stereo)
    - Sample rate configuration (16kHz, 44.1kHz, 48kHz)
    - Audio frame buffer size adjustment
    - Echo cancellation toggle
    - Noise suppression toggle
  - **Real-Time Audio Processing Pipeline**
    - Continuous audio recording when toggled on
    - Streaming audio data to speech recognition service in real-time
    - Voice Activity Detection (VAD) to detect speech vs. silence
    - Audio buffering strategy for optimal chunk size (100-500ms)
    - Automatic silence detection to end speech segments
    - Batch processing of completed speech segments
    - Interleaved recognition-translation pipeline:
      - Recognize speech in real-time as user speaks
      - Immediately translate recognized text (interim results)
      - Display partial translations as speech continues
      - Finalize translation when user stops speaking
    - Efficient memory management with circular audio buffers
    - Configurable VAD sensitivity to handle background noise
    - Audio preprocessing before submission (normalization, noise gating)
    - Queue management for pending translation requests
- Translation should be as real time as possible
- Output translation text should be rendered as a semi-transparent overlay that can be moved around and stays on top of other windows
  - **Overlay Window Management**
    - Frameless, always-on-top window positioned globally across desktop
    - Full draggability: click and drag anywhere on overlay to reposition
    - Persist overlay position/size in localStorage across sessions
    - Remember last used dimensions and location
    - Snap-to-edge detection when dragging near screen edges (optional)
  - **Visual Edge Indicators**
    - Visible border around overlay edges (1-2px) to show boundary clearly
    - Border color customizable (default: semi-transparent white/gray)
    - Optional glow/shadow effect around edges for depth and visibility
    - Corner handles/resize indicators for visual clarity
    - Hover effects on border to indicate draggable area
  - **Customization Options**
    - Background color picker (solid color or gradient)
    - Background opacity/transparency slider (0-100%)
    - Border color and width customization
    - Font family, size, weight, and color options
    - Text alignment (left, center, right)
    - Padding/margin adjustment around text
    - Background blur/frosted glass effect toggle (if performance allows)
    - Preset themes (light, dark, translucent, high-contrast)
  - **Overlay Controls**
    - Minimize/hide button to toggle overlay visibility without closing
    - Settings button to open customization panel from overlay
    - Close button to close overlay window
    - Optional floating control bar separate from display area
    - Pin/unpin button to lock overlay position and prevent accidental movement
  - **Display Features**
    - Real-time text updates with optional fade-in animation
    - Auto-scroll for long translations if text exceeds overlay bounds
    - Timestamp display of when translation was completed
    - Show source language and target language badges
    - Optional confidence score display from speech recognition
- Allow user to change settings through a user interface
- Support using API calls (such as OpenAI Whisper and DeepL) for speech recognition and translation
  - **Speech Recognition Engines**
    - Web Speech API (built-in browser API, no key required)
    - OpenAI Whisper API (requires API key, supports 99 languages)
      - Transcribe audio files or real-time audio streams
      - Higher accuracy than Web Speech API
      - Support for multiple audio formats (mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, webm)
      - Configurable language detection or explicit language specification
      - Timestamp granularity for precise timing information
    - Allow user to select preferred speech recognition engine in settings
    - Engine selection stored in user preferences
  - **Translation Engines**
    - Google Translate via Google Apps Script (free, no key required)
    - DeepL API (requires API key, enterprise-grade translation)
      - Support for 29 languages
      - Formal/informal tone selection
      - Glossary support for domain-specific terminology
      - Document translation for larger texts
      - Faster and more accurate translations than Google Translate
    - OpenAI GPT (requires API key, context-aware translation)
      - Support for 100+ languages
      - Custom system prompts for translation style
      - Context preservation for multi-sentence translations
      - Option to use gpt-3.5-turbo or gpt-4 models
      - Cost-effective for single word/sentence translations
    - Allow user to select preferred translation engine in settings
    - Engine selection stored in user preferences
  - **API Key Management**
    - Secure input fields for OpenAI API key (Speech Recognition)
    - Secure input fields for DeepL API key (Translation)
    - Secure input fields for OpenAI API key (Translation)
    - Show/hide toggle for API keys
    - Test connection button to verify API key validity
    - Clear error messages if API key is invalid or expired
    - Store API keys securely in encrypted localStorage
    - Ability to clear/delete stored API keys at any time
    - Warning indicator when engine is selected but API key is missing
    - Graceful fallback to free service if premium API key is not configured
- Ensure application works across Windows, MacOS, and Linux
  - **Build and Distribution Options**
    - Use electron-builder for creating platform-specific executables
    - **Windows Distribution**
      - Build standalone .exe installer (NSIS installer)
      - Create portable .exe for direct execution without installation
      - Code signing for trusted installation (if certificate available)
      - Auto-update mechanism via electron-updater
      - Use docker (electronuserland/builder:wine) to build on Linux
    - **macOS Distribution**
      - Build .dmg (disk image) for installation
      - Create .zip for direct distribution
      - Code signing and notarization for Apple security requirements
      - Universal binary supporting both Intel and Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3)
      - Auto-update support
    - **Linux Distribution**
      - Build AppImage for universal Linux compatibility
      - Create .deb package for Debian/Ubuntu systems
      - Create .rpm package for Red Hat/Fedora systems
      - Snap package for easy installation via snapcraft
      - Portable tar.gz archive
    - **Build Configuration**
      - Single `npm run build:all` command to build for all platforms
      - Platform-specific build scripts: `npm run build:win`, `npm run build:mac`, `npm run build:linux`
      - Version management tied to package.json version
      - Automated asset bundling and minification
      - Icon and branding assets for each platform
    - **Release Management**
      - GitHub Actions workflow for automated builds on release
      - Automatic creation of release artifacts in GitHub Releases
      - Changelog generation from commit history
      - Version tagging and semantic versioning
- UI should support light and dark mode
- Customize UI to look modern and sleek
- Ignore .backup directory
